title: Pycreus curvibracteatus
taxonomy: Pycreus curvibracteatus is a species of sedge, which belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is classified within the genus Pycreus, which is a group of sedges primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. The species is further classified into the subfamily Cyperoideae, which comprises various types of sedges. Within the Cyperaceae family, Pycreus curvibracteatus is one of the many species that exhibit similar characteristics and growth habits. The classification of Pycreus curvibracteatus is essential for understanding its evolutionary relationships and ecological roles within its native habitat.
